revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:石神社 (青梅市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Ishigami Shrine |image = File:Ishigami-sha (Ome).JPG |Location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|Ōme, Tokyo|en|Ōme, Tokyo|qid=Q237683|lt=Ōme, Tokyo|1=Ōme, Tokyo}} Futamatao 1-199 |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 47 |Latitude seconds = 45.4 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 13 |Longitude seconds = 31.7 |Map = |Enshrined deity = {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|qid=Q8191705|lt=Gen'ei Hime|1=Iwanaga-hime}} |Rank = |Founded = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alternate name = |Sacred sites = |Annual Festival = |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Q109594352|qid=Q109594352|1=Q109594352}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.795944444444444|N|139.2254722222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Ishigami Shrine''' (石神社<ref name="神社人">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://jinjajin.jp/modules/newdb/detail.php?id=1328 |title=石神社 |accessdate=2020-12-26 |website={{ill|Jinja-jin|ja|神社人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinja-jin|1=Shrine Person|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590698}} |publisher=国際教養振興協会 }}</ref>) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|Ōme, Tokyo|en|Ōme, Tokyo|qid=Q237683|lt=Ōme, Tokyo|1=Ōme, Tokyo}}, Futamatao, with its location detailed in the address.
== Overview == The location is Futamatao 1-199, {{ill|Ōme, Tokyo|en|Ōme, Tokyo|qid=Q237683|lt=Ōme, Tokyo|1=Ōme, Tokyo}}, Tokyo, situated near the JR {{ill|Ōme Line|en|Ōme Line|qid=Q385398|lt=Ome Line|1=Ōme Line}}’s {{ill|Ishigamimae Station|en|Ishigamimae Station|qid=Q8013316|lt=Ishigami-mae Station|1=Ishigamimae Station}}.<ref name="神社人"/>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|qid=Q8191705|lt=Gen'ei Hime|1=Iwanaga-hime}} (pronounced Iwanagahime)<ref name="神社人"/> and the {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|qid=Q327532|lt=Shintai|1=Shintai}} is regarded as a round stone,<ref name="図会">{{cite book |和書 |author={{ill|Mokuchin Ueda|ja|植田孟縉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mokuchin Ueda|1=Ueda Mōshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11540684}} |title={{ill|Musashi Famous Scenic Spots Illustrated|ja|武蔵名勝図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Famous Scenic Spots Illustrated|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名所図会の一覧|1=list of meisho zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11415434}} |date=1926-08-28 |publisher={{ill|Kōjirō Kobayashi|ja|小林幸次郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjirō Kobayashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |volume=多摩郡之部 巻11 |doi=10.11501/1172237 |page=25コマ目 |accessdate=2020-12-26 }}</ref> while it is also said that a {{ill|Mirrors in Shinto|en|Mirrors in Shinto|qid=Q11591009|lt=Sacred Mirror|1=Mirrors in Shinto}} is enshrined within the premises.<ref name="風土記">{{cite book |和書 |editor={{ill|Hitomi Ashida|ja|蘆田伊人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitomi Ashida|1=Koreto Ashida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17988362}} |title={{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|lt=New Edition Musashi National Fudoki Manuscript|lt_ja=新編武蔵國風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11503943}} (6) |date=1931-11-30 |publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11659206}} |series={{ill|The Great Series of Japanese Geography|ja|大日本地誌大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Great Series of Japanese Geography|1=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11435652}} |volume=10巻 |doi=10.11501/1214867 |accessdate=2020-12-23 |pages=176-177 }}</ref>
It is said that the shrine invited the deity of {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|qid=Q708859|lt=Ishikami Shrine|1=Isonokami Jingū}} in Nara Prefecture,<ref name="図会"/> though the time of its foundation is unknown,<ref name="風土記"/> and during the Edo period it served as the guardian shrine of Futamatao Village in {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|qid=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}, {{ill|Tama District|ja|多摩郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama District|1=Tama district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11430789}}.<ref name="風土記"/>
The second Sunday of October is celebrated as the date of the {{ill|Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Grand Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}},<ref name="神社人"/> during which the Futamatao {{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|qid=Q713866|lt=Lion Dance|1=Lion dance}} is performed.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.lib.fussa.tokyo.jp/digital/digital_data/folkloristics/pdf/0409/0001/0001.pdf |title=西多摩の民俗芸能 |accessdate=2020-12-26 |website=[https://www.lib.fussa.tokyo.jp/digital/digital_data/folkloristics/post-12.html Fussa City Library] |publisher={{ill|Fussa, Tokyo|en|Fussa, Tokyo|qid=Q317813|lt=Fussa City|1=Fussa, Tokyo}} |page=7}}</ref>
The {{ill|Ginkgo biloba|en|Ginkgo biloba|qid=Q43284|lt=Ginkgo Tree|1=Ginkgo biloba}} on the shrine grounds is a giant tree with a trunk circumference of 660 centimeters and a height of 25 meters,<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |editor={{ill|Environmental Agency|ja|環境庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Environmental Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1125558}} |title=4th Basic Survey on the Conservation of the Natural Environment: Japan's Great Trees and Ancient Tree Forests (Kanto Edition II) |date=1991-05-30 |publisher={{ill|Ministry of Finance Printing Bureau|ja|大蔵省印刷局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Finance Printing Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国立印刷局|1=National Printing Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q130035}} |isbn=4-17-319203-7 |url=https://www.biodic.go.jp/reports2/4th/kyoju/4_kyoju_kan2.pdf |accessdate=2020-12-26 |page=13の61頁 }}</ref> and is the subject of lactation worship,<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|author=児島恭子 |date=2018-02 |url=https://sgul.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/2860 |title=イチョウ巨樹の乳信仰─歴史研究の資料に関する課題─ |journal=Sapporo Gakuin University Humanities Society Bulletin|ISSN=0916-3166 |publisher=札幌学院大学総合研究所 |volume=103 |pages=73-85 |hdl=10742/00003086 |naid=120006399653 |CRID=1050845763134883328 |ref=harv |accessdate=2023-09-25 }}</ref> and it is said that drinking a decoction made from its bark promotes abundant breast milk production.<ref>{{Cite journal |和書|author=中西勲 |date=1995-10-15 |title=大きな木に会いにお出かけください |journal={{ill|Ome City Cultural Property News|ja|青梅市文化財ニュース|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome City Cultural Property News|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |issue=96 |publisher=青梅市文化財保護指導員連絡協議会 青梅市郷土資料室 |url=https://www.city.ome.tokyo.jp/uploaded/attachment/10348.pdf |accessdate=2020-12-26 }}</ref> This great ginkgo tree was designated as a {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|qid=Q23790|lt=Natural Monument|1=Natural monument}} by Ome City in 1957.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.ome.tokyo.jp/site/provincial-history-museum/3143.html |title=青梅市の文化財 |accessdate=2020-12-26 |website={{ill|Ome City Folk Museum|ja|青梅市郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome City Folk Museum|1=Ōme City Provincial History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11662005}} |publisher={{ill|Ōme, Tokyo|en|Ōme, Tokyo|qid=Q237683|lt=Ōme, Tokyo|1=Ōme, Tokyo}} }}</ref> [[ja:石神社 (青梅市)]]
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
